title: Apostasy — Leaving Islam (Riddah)
subtitle: Turning Back After Guidance Has Come

verses.arabic: كَيْفَ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ قَوْمًا كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ وَشَهِدُوا أَنَّ الرَّسُولَ حَقٌّ وَجَاءَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
verses.translation: How shall Allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief and had witnessed that the Messenger is true and clear signs had come to them? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

verses.arabic: وَمَن يَرْتَدِدْ مِنكُمْ عَن دِينِهِ فَيَمُتْ وَهُوَ كَافِرٌ فَأُولَٰئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
verses.translation: And whoever of you reverts from his religion [to disbelief] and dies while he is a disbeliever — for those, their deeds have become worthless in this world and the Hereafter, and those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.

verses.arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ارْتَدُّوا عَلَىٰ أَدْبَارِهِم مِّن بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْهُدَى ۙ الشَّيْطَانُ سَوَّلَ لَهُمْ وَأَمْلَىٰ لَهُمْ
verses.translation: Indeed, those who reverted to [unbelief] after guidance had become clear to them — Satan enticed them and prolonged hope for them.

brief_explanation: Riddah is the gravest outcome of following Hizb al-Shaytan — turning away from Islam after having accepted it. The Quran identifies it as a work of Satan, who beautifies leaving the truth for those whose hearts are diseased. Allah declares that all deeds of the apostate are nullified in both this world and the Hereafter, and that dying upon apostasy leads to eternal residence in the Fire. The Quran says Allah does not guide a people who 'disbelieved after their belief' — highlighting the self-inflicted sealing of the heart. This sin represents the ultimate rejection of the covenant made with Allah, and the Quran emphasises that it comes after the clear signs have arrived, leaving no excuse.
capital_punishment: The fuqaha of the four madhhabs (Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, Hanbali) unanimously hold that an apostate is given an opportunity to repent — typically three days. If they repent and return to Islam, they are not punished. If they refuse to return, the ruling of capital punishment is applied, based on the hadith 'Whoever changes his religion, kill him' (Bukhari) and the practice of the Sahabah. The Hanafi school holds that a female apostate is not executed but imprisoned. In contemporary Muslim-majority states, implementation varies significantly. This worldly ruling is separate from the Hereafter consequence, which the Quran states clearly: eternal Hellfire for dying upon riddah.
